description: Assigning `not` (the absence sentinel) to a non-optional type fires E-TYPE-041 (§42). `let x: string = not` binds the absence value to a plain `string`, but `not` is only assignable to an optional `T | not`, so the type system reports the mismatch. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §42 `not` is the absence value and is assignable ONLY to an optional `T | not` type; assigning it to a non-optional `T` is a compile error (E-TYPE-041).
